summary: A solid ’94 Rosemont soundboard capture that delivers a high‑energy set highlighted by High Time, Scarlet>Fire and a soaring Dark Star.
review: The recording is a clean soundboard source with only occasional static and a slightly thin low end, but it reproduces the band’s 1994 energy very well.  The show shines on High Time, Jack Straw, Scarlet>Fire, Looks Like Rain and an expansive Dark Star, though Easy Answers and a few late‑set moments feel flat.  Overall fans regard this as one of the stronger Rosemont performances of the era.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers praise the performance and key songs while noting only minor recording glitches; a strong show weight (70%) plus good soundboard quality yields an overall rating just above four stars.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp and well‑balanced for most of the set; a few listeners note static on tracks 4–5 and a slight lack of bass depth, but no major dropouts.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set opens strong with Jack Straw and rides a wave of high‑energy numbers (High Time, SMR, Sugar Magnolia) before tapering a bit at Easy Answers.  The second set erupts with Scarlet>Fire, a lengthy Dark Star and a heartfelt Miracle, delivering the classic ’94 Rosemont arc that fans love.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for fiery solos on High Time and Dark Star; several reviewers note his vocal tone was nasal in the spring‑’94 tour but his guitar work remains a highlight.
band_member_comments.Phil: Mentioned as having a ‘spiritual experience’ during the Scarlet>Fire segue; bass generally solid though a few listeners wished for more low‑end presence.
band_member_comments.Bob: Noted for lyrical “spray” on Dark Star and an acoustic contribution on Looks Like Rain.
band_member_comments.Keys: References to the new keyboardist era (Vince’s replacement) and Brent Mydland’s recent passing, with no specific complaints about the playing itself.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drums/Space segment praised as an integral extension of the Dark Star; overall percussion described as powerful.
